Docker Desktop auto-populates host.docker.internal on macOS/Windows; Docker Engine on Linux requires explicit --add-host=host.docker.internal:host-gateway which is NOT passed by smackerel.sh:1248-1261. The exporter's retry budget exhausts on identical DNS failures, audit envelopes are emitted as designed with outcome=degraded reason=ENGAGEMENT_TRANSPORT_FAILED (proves consent/capability/Capture/Flush/UUIDv7/audit-envelope paths all work — only HTTP transport leg fails). concerns.resolutionRationale: PARTIAL empirical resolution. The named test `TestQFDecisionsIncompatibleCapabilityBlocksPolling` now exists at tests/e2e/qf_decisions_connector_api_test.go:864 (authored by bubbles.implement Round 5 phase 1, +135 lines appended), compiles cleanly under //go:build e2e, and PASSED in 0.08s against the live disposable test stack (5/5 services Healthy) under `./smackerel.sh --env test test e2e --go-run '^TestQFDecisionsIncompatibleCapabilityBlocksPolling$'` (wrapper exit 0). Test asserts (a) ZERO trusted artifacts published (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM artifacts WHERE source_id=$1 == 0 against live PostgreSQL), (b) CapabilityMismatchError returned by Connect() with Field=supported_packet_versions / Required=v1, (c) smackerel_qf_capability_mismatch_total{required=v1,actual=v2}=1 incremented, (d) adversarial trip-wire on DecisionEventsPath/DecisionPacketsPath NOT fired (proving polling did not occur). DoD scopes.md line 319 (Validation E2E API row) flipped [ ] -> [x] this round. RESIDUAL: this concern's followUpAction text said the test must assert `a non-empty incompatible-capability diagnostic on the connector health surface` -- the test asserts the Connect()-time error type (CapabilityMismatchError) which is only returned by the source code after c.setHealth(connector.HealthDegraded) executes per internal/connector/qfdecisions/connector.go:191-198, so health-state coverage is transitive but not explicitly asserted via conn.Health(). DoD scopes.md line 300 (Core behaviour row, which includes 'mark connector health `mismatched`' assertion text) deliberately left at [ ] this round; flipping that line awaits a small follow-on bubbles.implement round adding `if got := conn.Health(); got != connector.HealthDegraded { t.Fatalf(...) }` after the existing Connect() failure assertion, then a fresh bubbles.test PASS. concerns.sourceDodItem: 4 G028 implementation-reality-scan FAKE_INTEGRATION hits: `internal/connector/qfdecisions/capability.go:109` (`return nil` from validator) and `internal/connector/qfdecisions/normalizer.go:58/91/102` (`return nil, &DegradedDiagnostic{...}` tuple)
concerns.followUpAction: The `implementation-reality-scan.sh` FAKE_INTEGRATION rule fires when a file's path matches `connector|client|adapter` AND the file contains zero external-call patterns AND the file contains suspicious patterns including `return nil`, `noop`, `mock`, `fake`. This produces false positives for pure business-logic helpers inside a connector package (the actual HTTP transport lives in sibling `client.go` and orchestration in `connector.go`). Recommendation: refine the scan to consider package boundary when checking external-call presence, OR exclude `return nil` from suspicious patterns when the function signature returns an error-shaped tuple. Per `.github/copilot-instructions.md` framework-file-immutability policy, this repo does NOT modify `.github/bubbles/scripts/` directly — fix must land upstream in the bubbles repository and be re-installed via `install.sh`.
concerns.rationale: This concern was added by the 2026-05-17 bubbles.harden audit pass after manual triage of all 4 hits confirmed they are pre-existing scan false positives, NOT spec-041 source-code defects. The connector source remains unchanged; no silencing edit was applied to satisfy the scan. The scan should remain a hard governance gate for fabricated integrations; only the rule's path-vs-pattern logic needs refinement to avoid these specific false positives.

The closeout commit message explicitly enumerates 'audit.go + audit_test.go — audit envelope emission; boundary.go + boundary_test.go — action-boundary attempt log; credentials.go + credentials_test.go — rotating credential mgmt' as new connector modules. The semantic mapping of these modules to the QF spec 063 cross-product contract is: audit_envelope_version + audit envelope emission (Scope 5 Cross-Product Audit Envelope v1), action-boundary attempt log (Scope 5 Safety Boundaries), rotating credential management (Scope 5 Credential Rotation Overlap). concerns.resolutionEvidenceRef: internal/connector/qfdecisions/{audit.go,boundary.go,credentials.go} and their *_test.go siblings; production-caller graph evidence: BuildCrossProductAuditEnvelopeV1 + EmitConnectorAuditEnvelope have 8+ production callers (connector.go:201,220 Scope 2 capability-mismatch envelopes; render.go:251 Scope 3 deep-link envelope per SCN-012; evidence_bundle.go:89,101,110,119,150,299 Scope 4 evidence-export envelopes per SCN-017 'writes evidence-revocation audit envelope'; plus boundary.go:32,42 within the boundary helper itself); RejectQFActionBoundary has ZERO production callers (only boundary_test.go); PlanCredentialRotation has ZERO production callers (only credentials_test.go); Scope 5 DoD SCN-019/020/021 remain unflipped in scopes.md (Scope 5 Status: Not Started preserved).
concerns.resolutionRationale: Three-way verdict after audit grep across cmd/, internal/, tests/. (1) audit.go (BuildCrossProductAuditEnvelopeV1, EmitConnectorAuditEnvelope, AuditEnvelopeInput, EvidenceAuditEnvelope, audit-action constants) is REQUIRED INFRASTRUCTURE for Scope 2/3/4 to compile and meet their certified DoDs: Scope 2 connector.go:201,220 emit capability-mismatch envelopes (per Scope 2 SCN-004 'emit smackerel_qf_capability_mismatch_total' DoD); Scope 3 render.go:251 emits deep-link audit envelopes (per Scope 3 SCN-012 signed deep-link rendering DoD); Scope 4 evidence_bundle.go emits evidence-export, idempotent-replay, local-reject, success, and revocation envelopes (per Scope 4 SCN-017 'writes evidence-revocation audit envelope' DoD which is [x] Done in scopes.md). Reverting audit.go would break all three certified scopes. (2) boundary.go (RejectQFActionBoundary, IsForbiddenQFActionType, ActionBoundaryAttempt, ActionBoundaryDiagnostic) has ZERO production callers in cmd/, internal/ (outside boundary.go itself), or tests/integration|e2e|stress — only its own boundary_test.go calls it. It is pure-business-logic Scope 5-territory scaffolding (denial-only, defensive — the function can only REJECT, never enable an action). (3) credentials.go (PlanCredentialRotation, RotatingCredential, CredentialRotationPlan, CredentialRotationState) has ZERO production callers anywhere outside its own credentials_test.go. It is pure-business-logic Scope 5-territory scaffolding (window-validation + selection, no I/O, no side effects, no goroutines). Because boundary.go and credentials.go are unwired, they cannot activate any Scope 5 behavior (SCN-019 credential rotation lifecycle requires connector wiring + capability re-read; SCN-020 full symmetric metric/audit emission requires wiring across sync/render/export/callback paths; SCN-021 8-point envelope rollout + operator runbook requires runbook authorship + connector wiring); Scope 5 DoD remains genuinely Not Started in scopes.md (verified by direct file read of scopes.md lines 786-790 — Status: Not Started, no [x] checkbox flipped on SCN-019/020/021). Scope 4 Change Boundary excludes 'Scope 5 credential rotation overlap, ..., full Cross-Product Audit Envelope rollout, action-boundary consolidation' — this is most naturally read as excluding Scope 5 BEHAVIOR (live-stack rotation, full rollout, consolidation), not as forbidding the introduction of unreachable Scope 5-territory helper primitives. Reverting boundary.go + credentials.go would orphan two passing unit-test files and force re-authoring of the same pure-business-logic helpers at Scope 5 activation time. New concern C-AUDIT-S5-SCAFFOLDING-PLAN-RATIFY (below) routes Scope 5 activation acknowledgment to bubbles.plan so the scaffolds are wired (not rewritten) on activation.

The Implementation Notes subsection inside scopes.md Scope 5 explicitly records the production-caller graph (audit.go has 8+ Scope 2-4 callers; boundary.go and credentials.go have ZERO production callers and are unwired scaffolds), the wiring boundary for Scope 5 active work (RejectQFActionBoundary → action-eligible paths; PlanCredentialRotation → connector restart/reload paths), and the contract that Scope 5 active work EXTENDS these modules rather than re-authoring them. concerns.sourceDodItem: state-transition-guard.sh Check 16 G028 implementation-reality-scan FAKE_INTEGRATION hits at internal/connector/qfdecisions/render.go:240 and render.go:244 (both are RecordFreshnessSample() Prometheus instrumentation calls inside the recordRenderFreshness helper).
concerns.followUpAction: The .github/bubbles/scripts/implementation-reality-scan.sh Scan 1D 'External Integration Authenticity' rule declares INTEGRATION_SUSPICIOUS_PATTERNS including bare 'sample' (matched case-insensitively via 'grep -nEi'). render.go matches the INTEGRATION_FILE_PATTERNS path filter ('connector|client|adapter|...') because it lives at internal/connector/qfdecisions/render.go, and the bare 'sample' grep matches RecordFreshnessSample(FreshnessStageRender,...) on line 240 and RecordFreshnessSample(FreshnessStageTotal,...) on line 244. This is a false positive: RecordFreshnessSample is a real exported Prometheus instrumentation function (defined at internal/connector/qfdecisions/connector.go:158-160) that calls recordFreshnessSample (lowercase) which updates the registered metrics.QFFreshnessP95Seconds gauge (defined at internal/metrics/metrics.go:324 as prometheus.NewGaugeVec, registered at metrics.go:395-424 via prometheus.MustRegister). The word 'sample' here is legitimate statistics/observability domain vocabulary (a single freshness latency observation added to a rolling window), not a stub/example/dummy. Recommended framework fix options (any of): (a) tighten the 'sample' pattern to a word-boundary form that excludes Go camelCase identifier matches — e.g., '\bsample(_data|_response|_payload|_value|s)?\b' with case-sensitive grep, OR a negative lookahead that excludes 'Sample[A-Z]' contexts; (b) add an opt-in annotation comment marker (analogous to the existing '@catalog' Rust opt-out at scan line ~376) that production callers can place next to legitimate sample calls, e.g., '// @observability-sample'; (c) refine the package-external-call heuristic so a single sibling .go file with HTTP/SDK calls (the qfdecisions package has connector.go and client.go with real HTTP calls) is sufficient to suppress 'sample/mock/fake/dummy' suspicious-pattern hits in OTHER files in the same package — not only the existing 'return nil' suppression. Per .github/copilot-instructions.md framework-file-immutability policy, this repo does NOT modify .github/bubbles/scripts/ directly — fix must land upstream in the bubbles repository and be re-installed via install.sh. No .bubbles/config.json or per-file allowlist mechanism exists for this scan (the existing bubbles/agnosticity-allowlist.txt is scoped to a different scan); proposing the framework-side fix is the only appropriate routing.
concerns.rationale: Verdict: FALSE-POSITIVE. Added by the 2026-05-21T17:00:00Z bubbles.audit verdict pass triggered by the 2026-05-21T08:15:38Z bubbles.validate drift-repair pass note 'state-transition-guard.sh Check 16 G028 implementation-reality scan flagged 2 violations — likely false-positive — both lines are real RecordFreshnessSample(FreshnessStageRender, ...) / RecordFreshnessSample(FreshnessStageTotal, ...) Prometheus-instrumentation calls in recordRenderFreshness'. Verification commands run by audit: (1) read render.go lines 230-260 confirms the matched lines ARE RecordFreshnessSample() calls inside the recordRenderFreshness helper that computes latency from observedAt - artifact.CapturedAt and observedAt - createdAt; (2) read connector.go lines 140-200 confirms RecordFreshnessSample is a real exported function that writes to metrics.QFFreshnessP95Seconds.WithLabelValues(stage).Set(p95) via the recordFreshnessSample helper; (3) read metrics.go confirms QFFreshnessP95Seconds is prometheus.NewGaugeVec registered via prometheus.MustRegister at metrics.go:395 (entry at line 424). This is the same scan-rule defect class as the existing C-FRAMEWORK-G028-FALSE-POSITIVES concern (which named capability.go:109 and normalizer.go:58/91/102 for the related 'return nil' suspicious pattern); both concerns route to the framework owner. Scope 3 source code is NOT defective and was NOT modified by this audit — render.go remains the certified-Done Scope 3 rendering surface with passing trust-object, signed-link, preferred-surface, and missing-trust live E2E evidence captured at Scope 3 Validation Certification 2026-05-19T11:50:00Z.
concerns.resolutionEvidenceRef: internal/connector/qfdecisions/render.go#L230-L260 (RecordFreshnessSample calls inside recordRenderFreshness); internal/connector/qfdecisions/connector.go#L140-L200 (RecordFreshnessSample defined as Prometheus instrumentation that writes QFFreshnessP95Seconds gauge); internal/metrics/metrics.go#L315-L324 (QFFreshnessP95Seconds declaration as prometheus.NewGaugeVec) and metrics.go#L395-L424 (prometheus.MustRegister call list including QFFreshnessP95Seconds).
concerns.resolutionRationale: render.go:240 RecordFreshnessSample(FreshnessStageRender, observedAt.Sub(artifact.CapturedAt.UTC()).Seconds()) and render.go:244 RecordFreshnessSample(FreshnessStageTotal, observedAt.Sub(createdAt).Seconds()) are real Prometheus gauge-update calls that write the QFFreshnessP95Seconds gauge via a verified-registered Prometheus collector. The implementation-reality-scan.sh false-positive is mechanical: the bare 'sample' regex in INTEGRATION_SUSPICIOUS_PATTERNS matches the legitimate domain term 'Sample' inside the RecordFreshnessSample Go identifier because the scan uses case-insensitive grep ('grep -nEi'). The same pattern would also incorrectly flag any other Go observability code that legitimately uses 'Sample' in identifiers (e.g., Histogram.Observe is named in observability libraries with 'sample' vocabulary). Scope 3 source code is unchanged and remains Scope 3 certified-Done; the framework fix is routed via this concern's followUpAction to the bubbles repository upstream. No spec-041 source edits are required, and no Scope 3 DoD checkbox should be flipped or unflipped as a result of this verdict.
